primary_diagnosis:
Acute myeloid leukemia, ID 01/2026
Classification according to WHO 2022: AML with CEBPA-Mutation
Classification according to ICC: AML with in-frame bZIP CEBPA-Mutation
At initial diagnosis:
Peripheral blood: Hb 9,4 g/dl, Plt 42/nl, WBC 62/nl, 88% blasts
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy from 01.01.2026:
Cytology: Detection of 72% myeloid blasts
FACS: CD45 low' SSC low, CD34+' CD117+' HLA-DR+' CD38+, CD33+' CD13+, CD7-, CD56+/-,
CD64+
LAIP in the blast gate: 91 %
LAIP of CD45+ cells: 82 %
Karyotype (BM/PB): 46, XX [20] normal female karyotype
FISH (BM): Exclusion MECOM-, KMT2A- and CBFB-Rearrangement; no Detection of t(8;21),
t(9;22), t(15;17); no deletions 5q, 7q, 17p
Molecular genetics (BM): NPM1 neg., CEBPA pos. (biallelic: in-frame bZIP-Mutation +
N-terminal Frameshift-Mutation), FLT3-ITD neg., FLT3-TKD neg., IDH1/2 neg., BCR::ABL1
neg., DDX41 neg., TP53 WT, MLL-PTD neg.
Risk score (after ELN): Favorable risk
question: Treatment initiation?
tumor_board_decision:
Treatment initiation with Daunorubicin/Cytarabin (7+3 regimen). With Detection of a
In-Frame-Mutation in the bZIP-domain of CEBPA, additional administration of GO. In
case of response no indication for consolidating allogeneic stem cell transplantation.

primary_diagnosis:
Acute lymphoblastic leukemia, BCR::ABL1-positive (common-B-ALL), ID 07/2022
At initial diagnosis:
PB: Hb 9,8 g/dl, Plt 95/nl, WBC 42,5/nl, 40% blasts
Cytology (BM): blast percentage 65%
Histology (BM): Blast infiltration of approx. 80%, marrow fibrosis WHO-Grade 1
Cytogenetics (BM): 46,XY,t(9;22)(q34;q11.2)[5]/46,idem,-7[12]/46,XY[3], Detection the
Philadelphia-translocation, subclone with monosomy 7
Flow cytometry (BM): 72% blasts; LAIP with ED: CD45 low, SSC low, CD34+, CD19+, CD20-,
CD22+, TdT+, CD10+, CD38+, CD33-/+
Molecular genetics (BM): BCR::ABL1 positive (p190, e1a2); FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2 WT
MRD Marker (PB): Molecular MRD markers established: IGH VH-JH clonal V4-34 J4, IGK
Vk-Kde clonal
Cytology (CSF): Cell count 2/nl, no detection of atypical cells
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted Therapy?
tumor_board_decision:
Pathogenic variant. Study option with KRAS-G12D- / MEK-Inhibitor (Trametinib)
recommended.

primary_diagnosis:
Suspected MDS, ID 01/2026
Initial manifestation with anemia
IPSS-M low, IPSS-R low
Bone marrow histologically and cytomorphologically mildly hypocellular with Dysplasia
of the
erythropoiesis, granulopoiesis and megakaryopoiesis as well as prominent
erythropoiesis.
No blast excess, blast percentage 4%
None ring sideroblasts detectable.
Immunocytologically no definite evidence of a myeloid neoplasm.
Molecular genetic unremarkable findings
Cytogenetically normal male karyotype (46,XY)
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
The suspicion of MDS can neither be confirmed nor excluded. Regular follow-up and
further workup the anemia. With symptomatic anemia or Hb <10g/dl Therapy with ESA.

primary_diagnosis:
CML ID 01/2026
ELTS-Score 2,935
Left shift, leukocytosis 210/nl, neutrophilia, basophilia, 13% blasts.
FISH: BCR-ABL pending
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Switch Dasatinib on Asciminib; alternatively Ponatinib, Recommendation for resistance
testing. Allo-SCT to be planned.

primary_diagnosis:
Acute myeloid leukemia ID 01/26
BM 01.01.2026: 80% blasts,
FACS 01.01.2026: CD33+, CD34+, CD13+, HLA DR+, CD117+, no Detection lymphoid markers
Genetics: NPM1 WT, FLT3 TKD neg., remaining results pending
FISH: no evidence of a MECOM, KMT2A or CBFB Rearrangement
ELN: final assessment pending
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: Start 7+3+GO. Referral Allo-SCT to be planned.

primary_diagnosis:
c-ALL, BCR-ABL positive ID 01/2025
PB 01/25: CD19+, CD10+, CD34+, TdT+, CD79a+, CD22+
FISH: BCR-ABL-Rearrangement t(9;22),
question: Treatment proposal? Allo-SCT is declined by the patient.
tumor_board_decision:
Allo-SCT currently not feasible. TKI (Ponatinib) to continue. If applicable, addition
of Blinatumomab.

primary_diagnosis:
TLBL ID 01/25
CT H/T/A: extensive mediastinal mass
Histology: T-cell precursor neoplasm in the sense of of a TLBL
FACS: CD4/CD8; CD7+
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
indication for allogeneic stem cell transplant conference. Bridging therapy with
Nelarabin +/- Cyclo.

primary_diagnosis:
Acute promyelocytic leukemia, AML M3 ID 01/26
Sanz risk classification: High risk
Initial blood count: L 5,00/nl, Hb 5,0 g/dl, Plt 5/nl
FISH: Detection of a translocation t(15;17)
Molecular genetics: PML::RARA pos.
FACS: CD45 low' SSC intermediate to high, CD34-' CD117+/- (low)' HLA-DR-' CD38+ (low),
CD33+' CD13+, CD64+ (99% in the blast gate, 85% the CD45+ cells)
Bone marrow aspiration:
Cytology: 70% promyelocytes, compatible with AML M3
FACS: CD45 low, SSC intermediate-high, CD34-, CD117+, HLA-DR-, CD38+, CD33+, CD13+,
CD64+ (95% in the blast gate, 95% the CD45+ cells)
Molecular genetics: PML::RARA pos.
Karyotype: 46, XX, t(15;17)(q24;q21) [7]
FISH: Detection of a translocation t(15;17)
question: Treatment initiation
tumor_board_decision:
Continuation of therapy with ATO/ATRA with high-risk APL under addition of HU 2g 2x
daily. No leukapheresis.

primary_diagnosis:
Acute myeloid leukemia, ID 01/2026
ICC 2022: AML with MDS-associated gene mutations (SF3B1)
WHO 2022: AML, therapy-related (t-AML)
L 1,6/nl, Hb 8,2 g/dl, Plt 68/nl, blasts 2%, LDH 195 U/l
BM histology: hypercellular, signs of dysplasia
BM cytology: 35% myeloblasts, trilineage dysplasia
FACS: 15% blasts, LAIP: CD45 low' SSC low, CD34+' CD117+' HLA-DR+' CD38+/-, CD33+'
CD13-, CD56+/-
Molecular genetics: NPM1 WT, FLT3-ITD/TKD neg., IDH1 neg., IDH2 neg., TP53 WT
FISH: unremarkable
NGS: SF3B1 Mutation positive (38,7%)
Karyotype: 46,XY,del(7)(q31q36)[6]/46,XY,del(20)(q11.2)[2]/46,XY[12]
Risk profile: adverse
question: Treatment initiation?
tumor_board_decision: Induction with Vyxeos, prospectively allogeneic stem cell transplantation

primary_diagnosis:
Acute lymphoblastic leukemia, common B-ALL type, ID 03/2024
At initial diagnosis:
Peripheral blood: Hb 8,2 g/dl, Plt 12/nl, Leukocytes 2,8/nl, 15% blasts
Bone marrow:
Cytology: 72% blasts
Histology: 90% blasts of a B-ALL. CD79a+, CD10+,
CD34+, TdT+, CD20(+) Ki-67 85%. Bcl6- CD23- CyclinD1- CD5- MPOFACS: CD45 low, SSC low,
CD34+, CD19+, CD10++
FACS: ED-LAIP (55 %): CD45 low, SSC low, CD34+, CD19+, CD10+, CD22+
Molecular genetics: BCR-ABL1 not detectable
Histology (reference pathology): 65 % infiltration B-lymphoblastic leukemia; MF-1
question: Treatment recommendation?
tumor_board_decision:
Relapse therapy with Inotuzumab ozogamicin; parallel intrathecal triple therapy for
treatment of the ZNS-Manifestation, after of a Cycle Inotuzumab systemic CNS-active
therapy (HD-MTX i.v. depending on general condition and blood count with or without
HD-Cytarabin); subsequently Continuation of therapy with Inotuzumab. CD19-CAR /
Allo-SCT to be considered.

primary_diagnosis:
Common B-ALL, ID 11/2021
At initial diagnosis:
Peripheral blood: Hb 7,2 g/dl, Plt 28/nl, WBC 3,5/nl, 12% blasts
Bone marrow:
Cytology: 88% blasts
Flow cytometry:
CD45 low, SSC low, CD34+, CD19+, CD20+/-, CD22+, CD10+, TdT+, CD33-, CD38+
FISH: unremarkable findings, no ALL-typical translocations
Histology: Interstitial and nodular infiltration, MF-0/1
Molecular genetics: BCR::ABL1 negative
question: Treatment indication?
tumor_board_decision:
With rising MRD positivity initially Treatment initiation with Blinatumomab. CAR-T /
Allo-SCT to be considered.

primary_diagnosis:
AML after lineage switch from pre-existing B-ALL (ID 03/2022), with
KMT2A-Rearrangement
Cytology: 28% monocytic blasts
FACS: 26% blasts in the Gate: CD45 intermediate, SSC intermediate, CD34-, CD117-,
HLA-DR+ (high), CD38+, CD33+, CD13+/-, CD64+, CD14-
Molecular genetics: NPM1 neg., FLT3-ITD/TKD neg., KRAS G12D pos., CEBPA neg.
Previous diagnosis: Pro-B-ALL, Initial diagnosis 12/2022
Cytology: 58% blasts.
Histology: Diffuse blast infiltration (85%), CD34+, PAX5+, TdT+.
FISH: KMT2A Rearrangement in 42% the analyzed interphase nuclei.
FACS: blast gate: CD34+, CD79a+, CD19+, CD22+, TdT+, MPO-, CD3-, CD7-, CD20-
BCR::ABL1 negative
question: Further Therapy?
tumor_board_decision: Enrollment in Menin-Inhibitor-study.

primary_diagnosis:
Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (MRC), ID 03.2023
Peripheral blood: anemia, thrombocytopenia, Leukocytes 11200 /µl, approx. 78 % blasts
BMA: AML (M2 after FAB)
Cytogenetics: Complex karyotype: 45, XY, del(5)(q13q33), -7, add(12)(p13),
der(17)t(5;17)(q13;p13) [8]/46, XY [2]
Molecular genetics: FLT3-ITD neg., FLT3-TKD neg., NPM1 WT, IDH1 WT, IDH2 WT, CEBPA WT,
TP53 mutated (VAF 45%), RUNX1 mutated, ASXL1 mutated
ELN classification: adverse risk profile
question: Relapse therapy?
tumor_board_decision:
Salvage therapy with FLAG-IDA VEN (as per cardiology consult if applicable, VEN+HAM)
and upon achieving a new remission 2. allogeneic stem cell transplantation.

primary_diagnosis:
Acute lymphoblastic leukemia of the B-cell lineage (common B-ALL)
blood count before start of treatment: L 0,41/nl, Hb 9,1g/dl, T 33/nl, LDH 198U/l
BM cytology: 79% blasts
FACS: CD45-, SSC low, CD34 low, CD19+, CD20+, CD22+, Tdt+, CD10+, CD38+; 89% the CD45+
cells
Molecular genetics: BCR-ABL negative, IDH1 positive
Cytogenetics: 46,XY[1] normal male karyotype (findings with limited interpretive
value)
FISH: biallelic Deletion 9p21 (nullisomy), gain of chromosomal regions 9q22 and 9q34
as well as in the subclone on tumor cells with gain of chromosomal region 5q32q33
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted Therapy?
tumor_board_decision: Targeted Therapy with Ivosidenib (+Azacitidin).

primary_diagnosis:
Acute myeloid leukemia, ID 09/2024
Classification according to WHO 2022: AML post cytotoxic therapy (AML-pCT)
Classification according to ICC 2022: AML with recurrent genetic aberrations
(inv(16)(p13.1q22)/CBFB::MYH11)
Initial risk score (after ELN 2022): favorable
At initial diagnosis:
o Peripheral blood: Hb 10,3 g/dl, Plt 89/nl, WBC 5,8/nl
o Bone marrow cytology: 52% blasts
o FACS (BM): LAIP with ED: CD34+/-, CD117+, HLA-DR+, MPO+, CD33+, CD13+; negative for
CD19, CD3, CD7, TdT
o Cytogenetics: 46,XY,inv(16)(p13.1q22)[17]/46,XY[3]
o FISH: Detection CBFB-Rearrangement; Exclusion KMT2A-Rearrangement, t(8;21), t(9;22),
t(15;17); no deletions 5q, 7q, 17p
o Molecular genetics (BM): CBFB::MYH11 Typ A transcripts positive; FLT3 neg., NPM1
neg., IDH1/2 neg., KIT Exon 17 D816V pos., CEBPA WT, DDX41 neg.
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted Therapy?
tumor_board_decision: Pathogenic variant. Enrollment in KIT-Inhibitor study.

primary_diagnosis: Primary testicular DLBCL ID 01/26
question: Treatment initiation?
tumor_board_decision:
Orchiectomy of the right testis. 6 cycles of R-CHOP and 2 cycles of HD-MTX with
adjuvant radiation therapy of the left testis / if applicable bilateral orchiectomy.

primary_diagnosis: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL)(NOS)
question: Treatment initiation?
tumor_board_decision: Treatment initiation with 6x Pola-R-CHP with an IPI of 4.

primary_diagnosis:
Extranodal marginal zone B-cell lymphoma of mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT
lymphoma), Stage I AE, ID 06/2025
- Nodal involvement: none;
-Extralymphatic involvement: stomach (H. pylori positive)
- B symptoms: none
- Risk factors: extranodal involvement, elevated LDH
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Initially H. pylori eradication. EGD follow-up in 3 months. Depending on response
(macroscopic, histologic, and clinical) evaluation Radiation therapy versus W&W.

primary_diagnosis:
Aggressive B-cell lymphoma, (Primary mediastinal B-cell lymphoma (PMBCL) (ID 05/2022)
stage IVBS
aaIPI high: Stage, PS, LDH
question: Further treatment?
tumor_board_decision:
Nivo / Brentuximab vedotin, with potential subsequent alloSCT. Alternatively,
Glofitamab +/- GemOx.

primary_diagnosis:
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma(DLBCL) (Activated B-cell type),
Stage II BE, ID 03/2024
- Nodal involvement: cervical, supraclavicular; Extralymphatic involvement: Mass in
paranasal sinuses
- B symptoms: Night sweats, Fever
- Risk factors: extranodal involvement
question: Indication for CAR-T cell therapy in early relapse?
tumor_board_decision:
Evaluate re-biopsy. If DLBCL confirmed, CAR-T indication 2nd line (Axicel). Bridging
with radiation therapy. Molecular tumor board indication.

primary_diagnosis:
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL-NOS, GCB type), ID 12/2021 stage IV A (pulmonary
involvement); IPI high (LDH 500, age, stage, performance status, extranodal sites),
transformed from a
Splenic marginal zone lymphoma, ID 12/2021 stage IVAE
Nodal involvement: Spleen; Extralymphatic involvement: Liver
Risk factors: extranodal involvement
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Recommendation for BiTE (Glofitamab versus Epcoritamab). Re-biopsy (persistent CD20
expression?) to be pursued. Molecular tumor board indication.

primary_diagnosis:
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) arising from follicular lymphoma (Follicular
large B-cell lymphoma (FLBL) per updated WHO classification), ID 12/2020 stage IVA
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: Recommendation for Glofitamab.

primary_diagnosis:
Follicular lymphoma (C82.1-4), ID 11/2020
Nodal involvement: abdominal, cervical, axillary, mediastinal
Bone marrow infiltration
Risk factors: Bulk > 10 cm
FLIPI: 4 (advanced stage, elevated LDH, Anemia Hb <12 g/dl, >4 lymph node regions
involved)
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted therapy?
tumor_board_decision: Recommendation for EZH2 inhibitor therapy (Tazemetostat).

primary_diagnosis:
Secondary diagnoses / Comorbidities:
Arterial hypertension
Type 2 diabetes mellitus
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted therapy?
tumor_board_decision:
Pathogenic variant: has been described in studies of Mosunetuzumab + Polatuzumab
therapy as effective. Alternatively, enrollment in CD79B-ADC trial.

primary_diagnosis:
Diffuse large B-cell (non-Hodgkin) lymphoma, ID 04/2023
EBV-positive
Stage: IVBE+S
Manifestations: bipulmonary, mediastinal/hilar, pleural, bilateral renal, splenic,
retroperitoneal, inguinal, osseous, bone marrow
IPI: 2 (stage, extranodal manifestations)
CNS-IPI: 3 (stage, extranodal manifestation, renal involvement)
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted therapy?
tumor_board_decision: Venetoclax-based therapy (e.g. ViPOR).

primary_diagnosis:
Hairy cell leukemia ID 01/2026
Core needle biopsy left humerus: Extranodal manifestation proximal left humerus
BRAFV600E positive
BM biopsy 01.01.2026: no evidence of infiltration
PB 01.01.2026: Hb: 15 g/dl; L: 4,8 G/l; Thr. 280 G/l; LDH 150 U/l
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Humerus biopsy confirming HCL. No further evidence in bone marrow biopsy. Currently no
further treatment indication. Recommendation for regular follow-up.

primary_diagnosis:
Marginal zone lymphoma ID 06/2025
Stage: Ann-Arbor III
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
In case of progression and CD20 loss, recommendation to switch therapy to
Zanubrutinib.

primary_diagnosis:
Hodgkin lymphoma ID 01/2026
Pathology: classical Hodgkin lymphoma
Stage: Ann Arbor IIBE
CT head/thorax/abdomen: Mediastinal mass
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Initial diagnosis of classical Hodgkin lymphoma. For stage IIBE, recommendation for
BrECADD. In case of PET positivity, radiation therapy to follow.

primary_diagnosis:
MALT lymphoma of the stomach ID 01/25
Stage IEA
Biopsy stomach: ID MZL: H. pylori negative
CT head/thorax/abdomen: No further manifestation
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: W&W. Repeat EGD in 3 months. R-Benda in case of persistence.

primary_diagnosis:
DLBCL ID 01/2025
Stage IIB, mediastinal bulk
IPI: 2
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Early relapse of DLBCL. Recommendation for CAR-T cell therapy. R-DHAP initially to
continue.

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgG kappa, ID 06/2025
ISS-Stage: III; R2-ISS: III
Treatment indication: Anemia, multiple osteolytic lesions, Hypercalcemia, Renal
insufficiency
question: Treatment initiation
tumor_board_decision: Dara VCD

primary_diagnosis:
Plasma cell leukemia with multiple myeloma IgG Kappa, stage III A per Salmon and
Durie, ID 01/2012
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted Therapy?
tumor_board_decision:
No variant on NGS. t(11;14) in current FISH findings. Therefore, Venetoclax-based
therapy recommended.

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgG kappa, ID 01/2026
Stage: R-ISS II
Treatment indication: Anemia, Osteolytic lesions, Hypercalcemia
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Continuation of bridging therapy with IsaKd. Referral to CAR-T outpatient clinic for
BCMA-directed CAR-T cell therapy in the further course.

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgG Kappa ID 01/26
ISS-Stage: III. R2-ISS: III
Treatment indication: Anemia, multiple osteolytic lesions, Hypercalcemia
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: Switch to D-VRD, HD Melphalan and ASCT.

primary_diagnosis:
Suspected Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia with IgM gammopathy 01/26
MYD88 negative
Whole-body CT and bone marrow biopsy unremarkable
No light chain restriction
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Complete diagnostics. Evaluation of therapy with Rituximab in case of persistent
hemolysis / R-Bendamustin upon diagnosis of Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia.

primary_diagnosis:
POEMS syndrome ID 01/2025, confirmed by sclerotic bone lesions, Polyneuropathy,
monoclonal IgA lambda, elevated VEGF
question:
Recommended management for isolated local progression BWK 2 with otherwise stable VEGF
and systemic remission?
tumor_board_decision:
Recommendation: Given imaging and clinically largely stable findings and a single
moderately progressive lesion in BWK2, local therapy via radiation can be administered
in this area. Further follow-up assessments should be performed via PET/CT.

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, IgG lambda type, ID 06/2025, from solitary plasmacytoma BWK 10
(01/2024), R-ISS II, standard-risk cytogenetics
SLiM-CRAB: disseminated Osteolytic lesions
question: Treatment initiation for initial diagnosis of MM from solitary plasmacytoma
tumor_board_decision:
Treatment initiation with Dara-VRd with continuation of anticoagulation. Reduction of
Lenalidomid dosage due to the recent PE in the first cycle. Principally eligible for
high-dose therapy, consolidation and maintenance therapy in the further course.

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgA lambda, ID 01/2025
Treatment indication: Light chain ratio >100, Osteolytic lesions, Anemia
question: Presentation per SOP. Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
We recommend a repeat high-dose chemotherapy and autologous blood stem cell
transplantation as a tandem transplantation.

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgA lambda, ID 08/2023
Treatment indication: Progressive focal lesions
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: KCD Therapy

primary_diagnosis: Monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance type IgG Lambda, ID 09/2024
question: Treatment indication?
tumor_board_decision:
In summary of the findings, there is currently a monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined
significance IgG type Lambda. In monoclonal gammopathy, there is no treatment
indication

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgG type Lambda, ID 11/17
Stage IIA after Salmon and Durie
Treatment indication: Osteolytic lesions
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: MEK Inhibitor Therapy

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, light chain type Kappa, ID 07/2021
ISS-Stage: III; R2-ISS: III
Treatment indication: Renal insufficiency, Osteolytic lesions
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision: Indication for CAR- Therapy. Referral to Cell Therapy Board

primary_diagnosis:
Multiple myeloma, type IgG Kappa, ID 01/2021
CRAB criteria: Anemia; Osteolytic lesions
Revised ISS: Stage II
Stage per Durie-Salmon: II
question: Recommended management?
tumor_board_decision:
Currently, there was rapid progression under Lenalidomid maintenance. Planning CAR-T
therapy with Cilta-Cel.

primary_diagnosis:
Plasma cell leukemia with multiple myeloma IgG Kappa, stage III A per Salmon and
Durie, ID 01/2012
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted Therapy?
tumor_board_decision: The variant is pathogenic. We recommend MEK inhibitor-based therapy.

primary_diagnosis:
Plasma cell leukemia with multiple myeloma IgG Kappa, stage III A per Salmon and
Durie, ID 01/2012
question: Pathogenic variant? Targeted Therapy?
tumor_board_decision: The NRAS variant is pathogenic. We recommend MEK inhibitor-based therapy.
